title: Which AI Brain Should You Pick?
subtitle: Opus, Sonnet, GPT-5, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek — what they mean and when it doesn't matter.

sections.heading: The AI Brain Market in 2026
sections.content: Claude (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku), GPT-5, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, MiniMax, and more. The market changes every month.

sections.heading: How to Think About Models
sections.content: Smart vs. fast vs. cheap — pick two. Opus is smartest. Sonnet is balanced. Haiku is cheapest. Match the model to the task.

sections.heading: When the Model Doesn't Matter
sections.content: For simple tasks, most models work fine. The model matters most for complex reasoning, long context, and nuanced generation.

sections.heading: The Cost Reality
sections.content: Opus: ~$15/M input tokens. Sonnet: ~$3/M. Haiku: ~$0.25/M. GPT-5: similar range. The price difference is 60x top to bottom.

sections.heading: Our Recommendation
sections.content: Start with Claude Pro or Sonnet. Move to Opus only when you need it. Use Haiku/Gemini for high-volume low-complexity tasks.

key_takeaways: Smart vs. fast vs. cheap — pick two; match the model to the task complexity
The price difference between top and bottom tier models is 60x
Start with Sonnet and only move to Opus when you need complex reasoning
